concerns.analysis: On page 6, the output contains a fabricated link object pointing to a 'mailto:' address that was scraped from page 4. This email address URL is wrapped around random body text that was not a clickable hyperlink in the source PDF.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: support the Carolina heelsplitter (Lasmigona decorata), a federally and state endangered freshwater mussel.” According to the report (Plain text with no hyperlink)
concerns.output_quote: {"type": "link", "text": "support the Carolina heelsplit mussel ” According to the rep", "url": "mailto:aeckardt@wildlandseng.com"}

concerns.analysis: Several critical and meaningful maps from the source PDF are completely missing from the HTML output and have been replaced by text placeholders. For example, the Project Vicinity Map, Project Service Area Map, and Current Condition Plan View Map are absent as `` elements.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 13 contains a large map titled "Figure 1. Project Vicinity Map".
concerns.output_quote: The HTML output lacks an `` element for this map, instead displaying a text placeholder: `[Image: A project vicinity map for the Running Dog Mitigation Site...]`

concerns.description:
Header/footer elements retained in output (26): Wildlands Engineering, Inc. • phone 704-332-7754 • fax 704-3; Wildlands Engineering, Inc. • phone 704-332-7754 • fax 704-3; Running Dog Mitigation Site
Monitoring Year 1 Annual Report
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=26, pages_affected=20/42. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML across most pages — pervasive pipeline failure. Screen readers will encounter duplicate artifacts on every page.

concerns.description: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
concerns.analysis: There are two notable text alterations. First, on Page 6, the output includes an errant hyperlink at the end of the page containing a duplicated fragment of text and a 'mailto' URL that does not exist in that location in the source. Second, on Page 39 in Table 10, the Height and Vigor data for a Quercus rubra plant in Plot 12 is incorrectly extracted as 'fee' and '4' instead of 'Dead' and '0'.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: 1) No link or text at the bottom of Page 6. 2) '12 Quercus rubra 1 Northern red oak 8.0 4.0 Dead 0'
concerns.output_quote: 1) {"type": "link", "text": "support the Carolina heelsplit mussel \u201d According to the rep", "url": "mailto:aeckardt@wildlandseng.com"} 2) 'fee' and '4' in the corresponding table cells.
concerns.location: Page 6 (end of content array) and Page 39 (Table 10, Plot 12)

concerns.description: Table caption coded as header row: 3 table(s) have a single spanning first row that is likely a caption/title
concerns.analysis: caption_as_header=3. Examples: p16: "TOTAL AREA OF BUFFER MITIGATION (TABM)"; p18: "Project Information"; p33: "Vegetation Performance Standards Summary Table". First row spans all columns — should be a <caption> element, not a header row.

concerns.description: 2 bare URL(s) in text not converted to hyperlinks: http://cvs.bio.unc.edu/protocol/cvs-eep-protocol-v4.2-lev1-2.pdf; https://files.nc.gov/ncdeq/Mitigation%20Services/Watershed_Planning/Yadkin_River
concerns.analysis: bare_url_count=2. URLs appear as plain text instead of clickable links. Per NCDIT criteria, link text not wrapped in href is a major defect.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 5 table(s) have a data row marked as header: "Appendix 1", "Yadkin - Goose Creek", "Activity or Report"
concerns.analysis: data_in_th=0, datarow_as_header=5. Tables use <th> for data values (misrepresenting data as headers) or have a data row mismarked as a header row. Screen readers rely on <th> to announce column/row context.

concerns.description: Repeated footer/header text (2x): "1430 s. mint st, suite 104"
concerns.analysis: Short text with footer-like pattern appears on 2 pages

concerns.description: Displaced hyperlinks: 2 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=2, examples=['mailto:aeckardt@wildlandseng.com', 'mailto:aeckardt@wildlandseng.com']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 7 table(s) with 3+ rows have no <th> header cells
concerns.analysis: data_in_th_time=0, no_th_tables=7. Minor semantic markup issues: tables lack <th> elements or use time values as headers. These affect screen reader context but are less severe than data misrepresentation.
